title: 2026 Summer Raised Bed Plan
assumptions: User location assumed to be central Maryland / Mid-Atlantic USDA zone 7 as of April 25, 2026.
Long side of each bed is plotted horizontally for readability.
Warm-season planting is assumed to begin after the main frost risk, roughly May 8-20, 2026.
Only the first 10 feet of the 6x30 bed is used for vegetables this summer; the remaining 20 feet stays in recovery cover.
Each plotted square represents 2 in x 2 in, and crop blocks are planning footprints rather than exact canopy spread.
Cucumber planting should use row cover or insect netting until flowering if practical.

beds.name: Bed 1
beds.notes: Main high-confidence summer bed in the better soil.

phases.name: May-July
phases.notes: Four staked tomatoes on the north side with basil on the south edge.

beds.name: Bed 2
beds.notes: Mixed high-attention bed: cucumbers stay on a simple north trellis and peppers hold the south half.

phases.name: May-July
phases.notes: Four cucumbers on the north trellis, six peppers below, with restrained support planting.

beds.name: Bed 3
beds.notes: Only the first 10 feet is pushed for summer vegetables; the remaining 20 feet stays in recovery cover.

phases.name: May-Summer
phases.notes: First 10 feet in capped bean strips, remaining 20 feet in buckwheat recovery cover with small insectary pockets.

placements.label: Bush Bean
placements.notes: Capped productive strip A; sow around May 10-20.

placements.label: Bush Bean
placements.notes: Capped productive strip B; use for staggered sowing or the second bean strip.

placements.label: Dill/Cil
placements.notes: Airy insectary pocket near the productive section.

placements.label: Calendula
placements.notes: Low insectary support patch; keep open, not dense.

placements.label: Buckwheat
placements.notes: Summer recovery cover on the remaining 20 feet; mow before seed set.
